core_mechanism: conformal-invariance constraint on a system tuned to a critical threshold: boundary observables are invariant under the full group of domain-preserving conformal maps (not merely rescalings), so a quantity in an arbitrary geometry is fixed by mapping it to a reference shape
brief_reason: The abstract computes crossing probabilities at the percolation threshold and shows them invariant not only under changes of scale but under all conformal mappings of the region, a larger-than-scale invariance used to derive predictions for specific geometries.
